key_insight: A reduced-integration virtual element formulation with scaled-boundary parametrization can cut each section to a single integration point while still capturing finite-strain nonlinear response reasonably well.
engineering_relevance: This offers a potentially cheaper and simpler stabilization strategy for large-deformation solid mechanics analyses, especially when mesh quality and element distortion are important.
method: Taylor-expand constitutive quantities about the sectional center.
Use scaled-boundary parametrization to enable analytical integration of the weak form.
Evaluate the formulation on finite-strain benchmarks including hyperelastic anisotropy and elasto-plasticity.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports several numerical examples, including a nonlinear patch test and an asymmetrically notched specimen, showing accurate response capture and inelasticity prediction.
watch_item: The patch test was not satisfied for higher-order shape functions, and performance was better when physical elements closely matched the assumed parent elements.
paper_title: Reduced integration with scaled boundary parametrization for virtual elements at finite strains
